fields.HowDataTransfer: g) How the data is transferred
fields.StartDateAssessment: h) Starting date of the assessment
fields.EndDateAssessment: i) Ending date of the assessment
fields.ReasonMassSurveillanceTelecommunications: Reasoning about mass surveillance of telecommunications
fields.ReasonViolationLocalLaw: Reasoning about violation of local law
fields.HighViolationLocalLaw: c) High probability of violating local laws
fields.ReasonHighViolationLocalLaw: Reasoning about high probability of violating local laws
fields.HighViolationDataIssue: d) High probability of violating local laws and the data at issue
fields.ReasonHighViolationDataIssue: Reasoning about high probability of violating local laws and the data at issue
fields.InvestigatingImporter: e) Local authorities would be interested investigating the importer
fields.ReasonInvestigatingImporter: Reasoning about Local authorities would be interested investigating the importer
fields.PastWarrantSubpoena: f) In the past 5-10 years the importer was already required to produce a warrant or subpoena
fields.ReasonPastWarrantSubpoena: Reasoning about producing warrant and subpoena in the past 5-10 years
fields.DataIssueInvestigation: g) Does the importer have reason to produced of the data at issue for an investigation?
fields.ReasonDataIssueInvestigation: Reasoning about produced of the data at issue for an investigation
fields.LocalIssueWarrants: h) Local authorities have the right to issue warrants or subpoenas
fields.ReasonLocalIssueWarrants: Reasoning about produced of the data at issue for an investigation
fields.LocalMassSurveillance: i) The type of the data at issue is in principle of interest for mass surveillance
fields.ReasonLocalMassSurveillance: Reasoning about the type of the data at issue is in principle of interest for mass surveillance
fields.LocalAccessMassSurveillance: j) Local authorities could ask for access to such type of data
fields.ReasonLocalAccessMassSurveillance: Reasoning about the local authorities could ask for access to such type of data for mass surveillance
fields.LocalRoutinelyMonitor: k) Other reasons why requiring the importer to routinely monitor the data at issue
fields.ReasonLocalRoutinelyMonitor: Reasoning about why requiring the importer to routinely monitor the data at issue
fields.PassMassSurveillance: l) In pass already required mass surveillance
fields.ReasonPassMassSurveillance: Reasoning about in pass already required mass surveillance
fields.PassMassSurveillanceConnection: m) Produce some of the data at issue in connection with such mass surveillance?
fields.ReasonPassMassSurveillanceConnection: Reasoning about producing some of the data at issue in connection with such mass surveillance?
fields.ImporterObligation: n) The importer subject to reporting obligation with the type of data at issue
fields.ReasonImporterObligation: Reasoning about the importer subject to reporting obligation with the type of data at issue
fields.LocalSelfReporting: o) The data at issue contains information to local self-reporting
fields.ReasonLocalSelfReporting: Reasoning about the data at issue contains information to local self-reporting
fields.ReasonPastSelfReporting: Reasoning about past 5-10 years self-reported to authorities
fields.AssessmentProduceReport: q) Does the importer has a reason to produce the data at issue
fields.ReasonAssessmentProduceReport: Reasoning about does the importer has a reason to produce the data at issue
fields.ConnectionTargetedAccess: e) ...in connection with targeted lawful access
fields.ReasonConnectionTargetedAccess: Reasoning about connection with targeted lawful access
fields.ConnectionSurveillanceTele: f) … in connection with mass surveillance of telecommunications, online services, etc.
fields.ReasonConnectionSurveillanceTele: Reasoning about connection with mass surveillance of telecommunications
fields.ConnectionSelfreportingObligations: g) … in connection with self-reporting obligations of the importer
fields.ReasonConnectionSelfreportingObligations: Reasoning about connection with self-reporting obligations

descriptions.toBeCompletedByExporter: To be completed by the exporter
descriptions.toBeCompletedByImporter: To be completed by the importer
descriptions.problematicLawfulAccessDerogations: If a problematic lawful access were to occur on the part of the importer as per Step 3, the transfer is nevertheless permitted if one of the derogations provided for by Art. 49 GDPR or the corresponding provisions of the CH DPA applies:
descriptions.targetedAccessInvestigations: (e.g., investigations by the police, state prosecutors and other authorities)
descriptions.exporterBeliefNoDerogations: Based on the responses of the importer and the analysis done, does the exporter have reason to believe that (i) the importer will during the assessment period have to produce the data at issue) and (ii) it will be unable to justify such lawful access by way of one of the derogations of Art. 49 GDPR ...)
descriptions.problematicTargetedLawfulAccessQuestion: ... a problematic targeted lawful access concerning the data at issue?
descriptions.problematicMassSurveillanceQuestion: ... a problematic mass surveillance involving the data at issue?
descriptions.problematicSelfReportingQuestion: ... a problematic self-reporting obligation (according to the Importer):
descriptions.encryptionInTransit: Is the personal data at issue protected with adequate encryption in-transit (i.e. when transmitted)?
descriptions.transferMechanism: Is the personal data at issue protected by a transfer mechanism approved by applicable data protection law (e.g., the EU Standard Contractual Clauses in case of the GDPR, approved BCR, or - in the case of an onward transfer - a back-to-back-contract in line with the EU SCC), and compliance with it and its judicial enforcement be expected, insofar permitted under the importer's law?
descriptions.ifAnswerNoEnterSafeguardDetails: If the answer is NO please enter details into the EU Standard Contractual Clauses or similar safeguard!
descriptions.lawfulAccessCompatible: Based on the legal analysis above, the lawful access laws in the country of the importer are compatible with EU and CH law
descriptions.lawfulAccessTargeted: … in connection with targeted lawful access (e.g., investigations by the police, state prosecutors and other authorities)
descriptions.massSurveillanceTelecommunications: … in connection with mass surveillance of telecommunications, online services, etc. (e.g., by intelligence agencies)
descriptions.transferIsLabel: Based on the answers given above, the transfer is
descriptions.targetedLawfulAccess: Targeted lawful access (investigation): Risk of the importer (a) receiving a search warrant or subpoena (i.e. order to produce documents) from the police, a state prosecutor or other authority investigating a potential violation of local law or development of relevance for national security7), or (b) otherwise individually becoming subject of surveillance or other lawful access measures by these authorities as part of an investigation (e.g., its phone lines and Internet connections being intercepted or its cloud provider or ISP being required to produce the importer's messages or data); only those forms of lawful access need to be considered that were found to be incompatible with EU and CH law in the legal analysis
descriptions.warrantsSubpoenas: The local authorities in principle have the right to issue to the importer or its providers warrants or subpoenas as described above for the type of data at issue
descriptions.violationLocalLaw: The local authorities issue such requests regardless of whether they really believe that a violation of local law has occured or that there is threat for national security (e.g., for political reasons or as a scheme of extortion)
descriptions.highViolationLocalLaw: The data subjects (of the data at issue) have a high probability of violating local laws and the data at issue would be particularly interesting to investigate these violations
descriptions.highViolationDataIssue: The importer has a high probability of violating local laws, and the data at issue would be particularly interesting to investigate these violations
descriptions.investigatingImporter: There are other reasons why the local authorities would be interested in investigating the importer (e.g., because it is considered of relevance for national security or economic espionage by the local government) and, therefore, requesting the data at issue from the importer or its providers
descriptions.pastWarrantSubpoena: In the past 5-10 years, the importer was already required to produce the type of data at issue following such a warrant or subpoena from a local authority (or, to the importer's best knowledge, one of its providers was required to grant access to the importer's data)
descriptions.dataIssueInvestigation: Based on the above and the legal analysis, does the importer have reason to believe that during the assessment period it (or one of its providers) will have to produce some of the data at issue for an investigation as described above?
descriptions.nonTargetedLawfulAccess: Non-targeted lawful access (mass surveillance): Risk of the importer receiving a request of an intelligence agency or other authority to participate in the routine monitoring of communications or other data)
descriptions.localIssueWarrants: The local authorities in principle have the right to issue to the importer warrants or subpoenas as described above for the type of data at issue
descriptions.localMassSurveillance: The type of the data at issue is in principle of interest for mass surveillance (e.g., because it contains large volumes of third party communications or third party communications that could be of relevance for national security purposes)
descriptions.localAccessMassSurveillance: The local authorities could consider the importer to be a provider that has access to such type of data (e.g., because it is offering a corresponding service or is contracted to process such data)
descriptions.localRoutinelyMonitor: There are other reasons why the local authorities would be interested requiring the importer to routinely monitor the data at issue for and on behalf of the government (e.g., because it is considered of relevance for national security or economic espionage by the local government)
descriptions.passMassSurveillance: In the past 5-10 years the importer was already required to engage in mass surveillance for the local authorities
descriptions.passMassSurveillanceConnection: Based on the above and the legal analysis, does the importer have reason to believe that during the assessment period it will have to produce some of the data at issue in connection with such mass surveillance?
descriptions.selfReportingToAuthorities: Self-reporting to authorities: Risk of the importer being required to self-report data to the public authorities for investigational purposes)
descriptions.importerObligation: The importer could in principle be subject to such a reporting obligation with regard to the type of data at issue
descriptions.localSelfReporting: The data at issue typically contains information that is subject to local self-reporting obligations (for the purpose of permitting the local authorities to investigate a matter; self-reporting obligations as part of prudential supervision are not in-scope)
descriptions.pastSelfReporting: In the past 5-10 years the importer already self-reported data of this type to the authorities
descriptions.assessmentProduceReport: Based on the above and the legal analysis, does the importer have reason to believe that it will during the assessment period have to produce the data at issue as described above?
descriptions.legalAnalysisLawfulAccessLaws: Legal analysis on the lawful access laws of importer's country

placeholders.dataExportersOrSender: Specify the data exporter(s) or the sender
placeholders.dataImportersOrReceiver: Specify the data importer(s) or the receiver
placeholders.dataAtIssue: Description of the data at issue
placeholders.dataTransferHow: Description how the data is transferred (e.g., remote access only)
placeholders.reasoningDataTransferProbability: Provide reasoning for data transfer probability

questions.DataTransferImporter: a) The data is transferred to the importer for the establishment, exercise or defence of a legal claim (e.g., data to be used by the importer [also] for criminal, administrative or civil actions)
questions.TransferToImporter: b) The persons concerned have explicitly consented to the transfer to the importer (e.g., because they have accepted the risk of a foreign lawful access or because the data originated in the country of the importer)
questions.TransferToImporterForPerformance: c) The data has been transferred to the importer for the performance or conclusion of a contract of the exporter with the persons at issue or in their interest (e.g., data of customers to be served by the importer)
questions.LegalGround: d) There are other legal grounds pursuant to Art. 49 GDPR
